code_paths.expected_solution: Core code paths: (1) Grafana alert scheduler: pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/schedule.go - the Scheduler type runs a tick loop that dispatches evaluation jobs per alert rule. When evaluation takes longer than the rule's interval, it logs 'alert evaluation took longer than expected'. (2) Grafana evaluation engine: pkg/services/ngalert/eval/eval.go - the evaluator executes datasource queries and compares results against conditions. (3) Alertmanager dispatch: dispatch/dispatch.go - the Dispatcher receives alerts via the API, runs them through the routing tree (aggrGroups), and dispatches to notification integrations with configurable group_wait/group_interval/repeat_interval timers. (4) Alertmanager notification pipeline: notify/notify.go - the DedupStage, WaitStage, and NotifyStage form a pipeline that deduplicates, batches, and delivers notifications through configured receivers.
evaluation_criteria: Must identify pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/schedule.go with specific details about the tick loop or Scheduler type
Must identify dispatch/dispatch.go in alertmanager with details about aggrGroups or routing tree dispatch
Must identify notify/notify.go in alertmanager with details about the pipeline stages (DedupStage, WaitStage, NotifyStage) or notification delivery
Must reference specific function or type names from the code, not just file paths

ownership.expected_solution: Ownership: Grafana side - the ngalert/schedule subsystem (pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/) owns alert evaluation timing, with the Scheduler and its per-rule goroutines managing when rules are checked. Alertmanager side - the dispatch package (dispatch/dispatch.go Dispatcher type) owns alert routing through the tree, and the notify package (notify/notify.go) owns the notification delivery pipeline including deduplication via the nflog (notification log).
evaluation_criteria: Must identify ngalert/schedule as the owning subsystem in Grafana (not just 'Grafana alerting')
Must identify dispatch and notify as the owning subsystems in Alertmanager
Must mention the Dispatcher type or routing tree from dispatch.go
Must mention the notification pipeline or nflog from Alertmanager

related_issues.expected_solution: Related files: (1) pkg/services/ngalert/eval/eval.go - evaluation engine that runs datasource queries, controls evaluation timeout. (2) pkg/services/ngalert/schedule/registry.go - tracks active alert rules and their evaluation intervals. (3) pkg/services/ngalert/models/alert_rule.go - defines AlertRule model with IntervalSeconds field. (4) alertmanager api/v2/api.go - API endpoint that receives alerts from Grafana, entry point to the Alertmanager pipeline. (5) alertmanager silence/silence.go - silence management that can suppress notifications even without user-created silences (auto-silences from inhibition rules).
evaluation_criteria: Must reference at least 2 files from EACH repo (grafana and alertmanager)
Must include files beyond the core code_paths (eval.go, registry.go, api.go, silence.go, or similar)
File paths must be accurate for the actual repo structure at the pinned version

severity.expected_solution: Severity: HIGH. Two independent failure modes compound: (1) Grafana-side evaluation delays cause alerts to fire late (10-15 minutes) when complex rules exceed their evaluation interval, creating a backlog in the scheduler tick loop. (2) Alertmanager-side notification failures cause fired alerts to never reach operators, due to dispatch routing mismatches or notification integration timeouts. Together these create a silent alerting failure where critical conditions are detected late AND notifications are dropped.
evaluation_criteria: Must identify both Grafana-side (evaluation timing) and Alertmanager-side (notification delivery) as separate failure modes
Must explain why the combination is worse than either alone
